title: Spicy Almond Butter Udon
subtitle: with Charred Brussels Sprouts
description: Smooth almond butter blends into to a deliciously creamy sauce with the help of tart lime juice and sweet maple syrup. The citrus-spiked sauce coats crisp pan seared vegetables over udon, a thick Japanese noodle that can stand up to the toppings.

steps.title: COOK THE UDON
steps.text: Place a large pot of salted water on to boil. Trim the Brussels sprouts and halve or quarter them. Peel the carrot and cut into 2 inch long sticks. Thinly slice the scallions. When the water comes to a boil, add the udon noodles and cook until al dente, about 6 to 8 minutes. Rinse noodles under cold water and toss with 1 tsp vegetable oil.

steps.title: MAKE THE SAUCE
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-low heat and toast the peanuts, about 4 to 5 minutes. Cool and roughly chop. Halve the lime. In a blender, combine juice from half the lime, garlic, almond butter, tamari, as much of the chili garlic sauce as you'd like, maple syrup, and ¼ cup water. Blend on high until smooth. Add half the peanuts, season with salt, and blend once more.

steps.title: CHAR THE BRUSSELS
steps.text: Place the same skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the Brussels sprouts and cook, shaking the pan often, until charred in places, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add ¼ cup water to create some steam, reduce heat to medium, and continue cooking until sprouts are bright green and just tender, about 3 to 5 more minutes.

steps.title: GET SAUCY
steps.text: Add the carrots and continue to cook until tender, about 4 to 6 minutes. Reduce heat again to low and add edamame, almond butter sauce, and ¼ cup + 2 tbsp water. Toss vegetables to combine, reduce heat, and bring sauce to a simmer.

steps.title: ADD THE UDON
steps.text: Add the cooked udon to the skillet, taste and season, and toss to combine. Stir in half the chopped scallion.

steps.title: FINISH IT UP
steps.text: Divide spicy almond butter udon between large plates and top with remaining scallion and chopped peanuts. Serve with lime wedges. Dig in!

tips: TIP: To trim the sprouts, slice the bottom and peel away any leaves that look "weathered."
TIP: Your sauce should be fairly smooth with little, textural pieces of peanuts.
TIP: If your sauce is too thick, add warm water by the tbsp to loosen.
* These items will be split between multiple steps.
